message:
raptorq: harden governance schema, SIMD tail handling, GF256 solver, and Track-E benchmark invariants

Four related hardening changes across the RaptorQ stack, all landing
together because they share the same Track-E benchmark artifact and
test_log_schema surface.

## gf256.rs — SIMD tail fallback for short slice pairs

gf256_mul_slices2 and gf256_addmul_slices2 would dispatch into the
X86Avx2 / Aarch64Neon kernels even when the paired slices were shorter
than a single SIMD register (32 bytes on AVX2, 16 bytes on NEON).
The unsafe kernels assume at least one register worth of lanes; very
short inputs hit edge cases in the shared-setup paths.

Fix: when min(len_a, len_b) is below the register width, fall back to
the sequential shared-setup path (or two separate scalar addmul_slice
calls) before touching the SIMD kernels. Keeps the hot path intact for
normal-sized buffers.

## linalg.rs — test: eliminate_row across pivot resizes rhs

Adds eliminate_row_target_after_pivot_resizes_rhs_and_updates_tail,
which exercises the Gaussian solver path where the target row is
shorter than the pivot row and has to be grown in place. Verifies
matrix tail, rhs contents, and stats.scale_adds are all updated in
one step.

## decision_contract.rs — test: canary_hold action without fallback

Adds exact_canary_hold_action_is_reported_without_fallback, which
pins down the decision contract telemetry for a governance snapshot
where CANARY_HOLD strictly dominates CONTINUE on expected loss:
chosen_action must be "canary_hold", deterministic_fallback_triggered
must be false, and the reason must be "none".

## test_log_schema.rs — permille sum, score complement, contributor consistency

Tightens the governance validator in the Track-E e2e log schema:

- state_posterior map values must sum to exactly 1000 permille
- confidence_score / uncertainty_score are validated as permille
  (0..=1000) and must be complementary (score + uncertainty == 1000)
- top_evidence_contributors entries must be internally consistent
  (weights sum, ordering, non-duplicated ids)

These were previously permissive unsigned-integer checks. The new
helpers short-circuit cleanly when the field is absent so optional
governance sections continue to validate.

## raptorq_perf_invariants.rs — e5 benchmark artifact structural invariants

Adds e5_bench_artifact_top_level_runs_exercise_simd_surface, which
reads raptorq_track_e_gf256_bench_v1.json from disk and asserts the
artifact always carries baseline/auto/rollback runs, each invoked via
rch exec offload against cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark.
Guards against accidental artifact drift that would silently take the
benchmark off the SIMD surface we rely on for Track-E regression
detection.

## artifacts/raptorq_track_e_gf256_bench_v1.json

Refreshed benchmark artifact matching the new schema and kept in
sync with the e5 invariant test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
